stemHtml: When planning an IFR flight, the pilot notices that there is a warm front on his/her route. It is not possible to fly around it and the performance of the aircraft does not allow to climb over it. How should he/she plan his/her route?

options.a: Plan his/her route through the warm front on the shortest way possible.
options.c: Descend low as possible, staying at 500 ft AGL, even if it means flying below MSA and entering IMC.
options.d: Fly along searching for an opening.
